neighborhoods.name: City of Fairfax / Old Town
neighborhoods.description: Walkable historic core around the Fairfax County government complex with Main Street restaurants and the Old Town Hall arts venue

housing.predominantType: Townhomes, mid-rise apartments, and historic homes

neighborhoods.character_notes: The historic and commercial heart of Fairfax. Most walkable area with best access to cultural attractions, restaurants, and public services.

neighborhoods.name: Fairfax Station / Burke
neighborhoods.description: Established residential area around Lake Accotink Park with Burke Centre VRE station and large-lot single-family homes
neighborhoods.character: Established suburban, lake park, VRE access

housing.predominantType: Single-family homes on wooded half-acre lots

neighborhoods.character_notes: Established residential area offering a quieter lifestyle while maintaining reasonable access to Fairfax's center and services.

neighborhoods.name: Mantua / Pickett's Reserve
neighborhoods.description: Older mid-century split-levels and ramblers with mature trees and more accessible price points than newer Fairfax County developments
neighborhoods.character_notes: More affordable option for budget-conscious retirees. May require a car or longer transit times to reach central amenities.

neighborhoods.name: Vienna / Oakton
neighborhoods.description: Top-rated school zone with Vienna Metro station, walkable Vienna town center, and Wolf Trap National Park nearby

housing.predominantType: Single-family homes, townhomes, and Metro-adjacent condos

neighborhoods.character_notes: Family-friendly area with good schools, parks, and community amenities. Popular with active retirees and families.
